critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

The full-autonomy, no-override, all-400-stores deployment has two genuine flaws:

1. LOW-VOLUME STORE PERFORMANCE: 19.4% MAPE in the bottom quintile stores is 2.3x the overall model. For perishable goods ordered autonomously, this level of error creates waste and stockout risks. These stores need restricted deployment or better model performance before full autonomy.

2. NO OVERRIDE CAPABILITY: The architecture completely removes human intervention capability for perishable order management. This eliminates the ability to respond to exceptional demand events, supply disruptions, or known model limitations. A minimum exception process is required for perishable goods.

Verdict: MIXED. High-volume stores support full autonomy; low-volume stores and exception handling require deployment design modifications.
